task_completion.description: Navigate to the Student Custom Fields configuration page in OpenSIS
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenSIS web interface is visible, and the user has navigated to the setup section for Student Fields or Custom Fields (typically under Setup > Students).

task_completion.description: Create a new custom field category named 'Transportation'
task_completion.visual_evidence: A form or list showing a category specifically named 'Transportation' being added or already present in the custom field categories list.

task_completion.description: Start the process of adding a new custom field under the 'Transportation' category
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'New Field' form or dialog is open, with the category 'Transportation' selected from a dropdown or associated with the new field.

task_completion.description: Name the new custom field 'Bus Route Number'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The exact text 'Bus Route Number' is entered into the field name or title input box.

task_completion.description: Set the field type to 'Text Box' (or Text)
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dropdown, radio button, or selection menu for the field type has 'Text Box', 'Text', or a similar standard text input option selected.

task_completion.description: Save the new custom field and verify it appears in the system
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message or a summary list of custom fields displaying 'Bus Route Number' under the 'Transportation' category with a 'Text Box' (or Text) type.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenSIS web interface to create the fields rather than directly modifying the database via terminal commands
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the OpenSIS web UI in a browser. There should be no terminal windows showing direct MySQL INSERT commands for the custom fields.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual OpenSIS application
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows a local or appropriate URL for the OpenSIS instance, and the UI matches the real OpenSIS web application, not a text editor or mock HTML file.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a pre-written script to bypass the UI configuration
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of terminal windows running Python, PHP, or Bash scripts that directly insert the records into the database to bypass manual GUI configuration.

privileged_info_for_vlm: OpenSIS v9.2 uses the 'student_field_categories' table for student field categories. For custom field types, OpenSIS accepts values including 0 (Text) or string representations like 'text' or 'text box'. The VLM should accept 'Text Box' or 'Text' as valid selections in the GUI for the field type.
